title: Best LLM for Finance Workloads: Compare Prices Pricing Grid
description: Picking an LLM for finance workloads (report analysis, compliance, market summarization) via TokenRate's Compare Prices grid.

sections.heading: Why Finance LLM Picking Has Its Own Logic
sections.body: TokenRate's new [Compare Prices grid](/tools/compare-prices) puts every model's per-token rates, context window, and quality score in a single side-by-side view. The point: stop flipping between provider pricing pages and OpenRouter tabs. You pick a provider dropdown, check the models you want, repeat for each provider, and the grid stacks every pick into one comparison table. LLM picking for Finance workloads follows different rules than a generic SaaS chatbot. Finance workloads have strict accuracy and auditability requirements. The wrong LLM pick can produce compliance incidents — model selection is a governance decision, not just a cost decision. The [Compare Prices grid](/tools/compare-prices) is the right starting point because it puts the cost-quality-context tradeoff on one screen — the three dimensions that Finance teams care about. Pair this with [flagship/balanced/fast/reasoning LLM tiers](/blog/flagship-balanced-fast-reasoning-llm-tiers), [Arena AI leaderboard Elo scores explained](/blog/arena-ai-leaderboard-elo-scores-explained), and [how LLM quality scores are calculated](/blog/how-llm-quality-scores-are-calculated).

sections.heading: Finance Workload Characteristics
sections.body: Document analysis (10-K filings, contracts), structured extraction (financial line items), and reasoning over numeric tables. Quality floor is high; context windows must fit reports. That profile narrows the field of candidate models significantly. In the [Compare Prices grid](/tools/compare-prices), filter by the quality column first, then by the context window column second, then read the cost columns. For Finance, the typical sweet spot is flagship tier with strong numeric reasoning (Opus 4, GPT-5, o3) for high-stakes; Sonnet 4.7 for routine extraction.

sections.heading: Top Picks for Finance
sections.body: **GPT-5** (OpenAI): $1.25 / $10.00, Q82, 200K ctx — customer-facing premium experiences, complex writing/code, low-volume high-value queries where the cost is dwarfed by what the answer is worth. **Claude Opus 4** (Anthropic): $15.00 / $75.00, Q85, 200K ctx — customer-facing premium experiences, complex writing/code, low-volume high-value queries where the cost is dwarfed by what the answer is worth. **Claude Sonnet 4.7** (Anthropic): $3.00 / $15.00, Q80, 200K ctx — production routing default — chatbots, RAG answer synthesis, structured output, anything that ships to real users at scale. Tick all three in [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices) for the side-by-side view. The grid shows the Value column for each so the production-default candidate is visible without manual math.

sections.heading: Gotchas Specific to Finance
sections.body: Finance workloads sometimes trip on the "I'll just pick the flagship" reflex — paying for capability that the workload doesn't actually use. The Compare Prices grid is the antidote: visible tradeoffs make over-paying obvious. For broader cost-control patterns, see [token budgeting for production AI apps](/blog/token-budgeting-for-production-ai-apps).

sections.heading: Operationalizing the Pick
sections.body: Once you've narrowed to a top pick from the [Compare Prices grid](/tools/compare-prices), run your projected token volume through [/tools/api-cost-estimator](/tools/api-cost-estimator). For Finance teams, a typical month is 10-100M tokens/month for a mid-size fund or fintech analytics product. Pricing is pulled live from [OpenRouter's models endpoint](https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models) and revalidated every 60 minutes via Next.js's incremental cache, so the grid you see is at most an hour stale. Quality scores blend [Arena AI](https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard) Elo with [Artificial Analysis](https://artificialanalysis.ai) intelligence-index data on the same cadence. Open [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices) now, pick your provider dropdowns, and pin the shortlist that matches your workload.

faq.question: How do I open the Compare Prices grid?
faq.answer: Two ways: click the 'Compare Prices' tab at the top of the calculator card on the home page, or navigate directly to /tools/compare-prices. The standalone page is also linked from the main navigation under 'Tools'.

faq.question: Can I share my comparison with teammates?
faq.answer: Yes — the page URL captures the current state. Send the link in Slack and your teammate sees the same grid. Useful for procurement and architecture-review meetings.

faq.question: Is the data live or cached?
faq.answer: Live from OpenRouter (prices) and a blended Arena AI + Artificial Analysis pipeline (quality), refreshed on a 60-minute incremental cache. So the grid is at most an hour stale.

faq.question: Where do I go after the grid to project monthly cost?
faq.answer: Once you've picked a winner, go to /tools/api-cost-estimator and plug in the model + your expected monthly token volume. The estimator does the per-1M math against your real workload mix.

ctaText: Open [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices) now, pick your provider dropdowns, and pin the shortlist that matches your workload.
